channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: After 80 years, the U.S.-U.K. 'special relationship' has changed under Trump
item.description: Winston Churchill coined the phrase "special relationship" 80 years ago. Now, his grandson says Trump is damaging U.S.-U.K. ties, and diplomats warn a divorce may be in the works.
item.content:encoded: Winston Churchill coined the phrase "special relationship" 80 years ago. Now, his grandson says Trump is damaging U.S.-U.K. ties, and diplomats warn a divorce may be in the works.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: A rift between Spain and Trump widens over Spanish opposition to the Iran war
item.description: The Spanish government reiterated it would not let U.S. forces use two joint military bases in Spain as the U.S.-Israeli war in Iran escalates, widening a rift with the Trump administration.
item.content:encoded:
The Spanish government reiterated it would not let U.S. forces use two joint military bases in Spain as the U.S.-Israeli war in Iran escalates, widening a rift with the Trump administration.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: Miguel Macias

item.title: House to vote on Trump's war powers. And, Minnesota sues over halted Medicaid funding
item.description: The House is set to vote today on whether to constrain President Trump's authority to continue to wage war on Iran. And, Minnesota sues the Trump administration over halted Medicaid funding.
item.content:encoded:
The House is set to vote today on whether to constrain President Trump's authority to continue to wage war on Iran. And, Minnesota sues the Trump administration over halted Medicaid funding.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump wants to help pick Iran's next leader as the war stretches into Day 6
item.description: The U.S. and Israel say they conducted new strikes inside Iran overnight, targeting ballistic missile launchers. Iran claims it struck a U.S. oil tanker in the northern Persian Gulf.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Israel say they conducted new strikes inside Iran overnight, targeting ballistic missile launchers. Iran claims it struck a U.S. oil tanker in the northern Persian Gulf.

(Image credit: Ali Ihsan Ozturk)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Middle East conflict enters Day 6, clerics convene to replace Iran's supreme leader, China to send special envoy to help mediate the US-Israel-Iran war.
item.content:encoded: Middle East conflict enters Day 6, clerics convene to replace Iran's supreme leader, China to send special envoy to help mediate the US-Israel-Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Oscar-nominated Iranian dissident warns against 'repeat of the past'
item.description: Mehdi Mahmoudian worries Iran could experience an endless cycle of violence unless — or until — it achieves democracy.
item.content:encoded: Mehdi Mahmoudian worries Iran could experience an endless cycle of violence unless — or until — it achieves democracy.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Ukraine offers drone expertise to Gulf amid Iran strikes
item.description: As Iran launches its Shahed drones at Gulf nations in retaliation for strikes by the U.S. and Israel, Ukraine offers its expertise at downing the drones Russia has lobbed at its cities for years.
item.content:encoded: As Iran launches its Shahed drones at Gulf nations in retaliation for strikes by the U.S. and Israel, Ukraine offers its expertise at downing the drones Russia has lobbed at its cities for years.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: China offers to mediate in US-Israel-Iran war
item.description: China says it will send a special envoy to the Middle East to help mediate the US-Israel-Iran war, as Chinese political elites gather in Beijing for the annual "Two Sessions."
item.content:encoded: China says it will send a special envoy to the Middle East to help mediate the US-Israel-Iran war, as Chinese political elites gather in Beijing for the annual "Two Sessions."
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: China sets a lower economic growth target of 4.5% to 5% for 2026 as challenges loom
item.description: China has signaled continuity rather than change for its economy, setting a slightly lower target for growth this year in the midst of a property slump and other headwinds at home and growing uncertainty abroad.
item.content:encoded:
China has signaled continuity rather than change for its economy, setting a slightly lower target for growth this year in the midst of a property slump and other headwinds at home and growing uncertainty abroad.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Carney says he backs strikes on Iran 'with some regret' as world order frays
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says he supports the strikes on Iran "with some regret" as they represent an extreme example of a rupturing world order.
item.content:encoded:
Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says he supports the strikes on Iran "with some regret" as they represent an extreme example of a rupturing world order.

(Image credit: Adrian Wyld/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
